examples.title: Simple Desert Classification Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to classify regions based on precipitation
examples.code:
FUNCTION classify_region(precipitation):
 IF precipitation < 250 THEN
 RETURN "Desert"
 ELSE IF precipitation < 500 THEN
 RETURN "Semi-arid"
 ELSE IF precipitation < 1000 THEN
 RETURN "Sub-humid"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Humid"
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example usage
annual_rainfall = 150
region_type = classify_region(annual_rainfall)
PRINT "Region type:", region_type
examples.explanation: This algorithm classifies a region as desert if annual precipitation is less than 250mm, which is the standard definition used in climatology.

examples.title: Desert Animal Water Conservation Simulation
examples.description: Simulating water balance in desert animals
examples.code:
CLASS DesertAnimal:
 water_reserve = 0
 water_loss_rate = 0
 
 FUNCTION init(initial_water):
 water_reserve = initial_water
 
 FUNCTION calculate_water_loss(activity_level, temperature):
 base_loss = 10
 activity_factor = activity_level * 2
 temp_factor = temperature / 30
 water_loss_rate = base_loss + activity_factor + temp_factor
 RETURN water_loss_rate
 
 FUNCTION survive(days):
 FOR day IN 1 TO days:
 water_loss = calculate_water_loss(0.5, 35)
 water_reserve = water_reserve - water_loss
 IF water_reserve <= 0 THEN
 PRINT "Animal died on day", day
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 PRINT "Animal survived"
 RETURN TRUE
 END FUNCTION
END CLASS
examples.explanation: This simulation models how desert animals lose water based on activity and temperature, helping understand water conservation adaptations.

examples.title: Desertification Risk Assessment
examples.description: Calculating desertification risk based on environmental factors
examples.code:
FUNCTION calculate_desertification_risk(precipitation, vegetation_cover, soil_quality, human_activity):
 // Each factor weighted
 precipitation_score = (250 - precipitation) / 250
 vegetation_score = 1 - (vegetation_cover / 100)
 soil_score = 1 - (soil_quality / 100)
 human_factor = human_activity / 10
 
 // Weighted average
 risk_score = (precipitation_score * 0.3) + (vegetation_score * 0.3) + (soil_score * 0.2) + (human_factor * 0.2)
 
 IF risk_score > 0.7 THEN
 RETURN "High Risk"
 ELSE IF risk_score > 0.4 THEN
 RETURN "Moderate Risk"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Low Risk"
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example: Assess a region
risk = calculate_desertification_risk(200, 20, 50, 7)
PRINT "Desertification Risk:", risk
examples.explanation: This model helps predict which regions are at risk of becoming deserts based on environmental and human factors.
